findings.content: Persistent grocery lists require a new microservice or significant extension of the current cart service. The cart service is shared across food and grocery — changes have blast radius.
findings.source: Architecture review of cart-service repo and deployment topology

findings.content: Delivery slot confidence visualization can be built in 2 sprints using existing slot allocation API. No backend changes required — purely frontend.
findings.source: Slot allocation API documentation review + frontend codebase audit

findings.content: The InstaShop integration already has a list management component that could be adapted for substitution preferences with 40% less effort than building from scratch.

artifacts_produced.description: Engineering spec for delivery slot confidence visualization — 2-sprint estimate

cross_references.reason: Engineering feasibility confirms Design's slot visualization opportunity is low-effort

self_review.self_check_notes: Feasibility grounded in actual code review of cart-service and slot allocation API. Estimates broken down by component. Blast radius specified for cart service.
self_review.review_feedback: Feasibility assessments are grounded in actual code review. InstaShop reuse opportunity needs validation with the integration team.
revisions_made: Added blast radius note for cart service changes
